front: Poem: the Redcrosse Knight is tricked by Archimago; slays Error; meets Fradubio turned into a tree by Duessa; Gloriana is the title figure
Poem: introduced a stanza of 8 lines iambic pentameter + alexandrine (ABABBCBC C); later used by Byron and Keats
Poem: Sonnet 75 — 'One day I wrote her name upon the strand'; the tide washes it away; 'Our love shall live, and later life renew'
Poem: opens 'Happy ye leaves when as those lily hands'; 89-sonnet cycle using interlocking quatrains dedicated to Elizabeth Boyle
Poem: 24-stanza marriage ode; each stanza ends 'The woods shall to me answer, and my Eccho ring'
Poem: 12 eclogues tracing pastoral life through Colin Clout; annotated by the mysterious 'E.K.'; uses deliberate archaic spelling
Poem: the female knight Britomart sees 'be bold, be bold' above a door in the sorcerer Busirane's house
